sourceMovie.title: Highlander
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Highlander works as a adventure movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The adventure and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Highlander clicked for you, The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring (2001) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King

faq.question: What should I watch after Highlander?
faq.answer: Try The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring, The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King, The Lord of the Rings: The Two Towers—each shares DNA with Highlander while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Highlander?
faq.answer: Highlander (1986) is best described as Adventure, Action, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Highlander part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Highlander Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
